Name: Venafi TPP - Import Certificate into Octopus
Description:
This step template will authenticate against a Venafi TPP instance using an existing OAuth access token, export a certificate by its Distinguished Name (DN), and then import the certificate into the Octopus certificate store.

The export is achieved using the VenafiPS PowerShell module's [Export-VenafiCertificate](https://venafips.readthedocs.io/en/latest/functions/Export-VenafiCertificate/) function.

---

**Export options:**

- Provide the distinguished name (DN) path to the certificate.
- Choose from the following export formats:
  - `Base64`
  - `Base64 (PKCS #8)`
  - `DER`
  - `PKCS #12` 
- *Optional* - Include the full certificate chain in the export.
- *Optional* - Friendly name (Label or alias) to use. Permitted with `Base64` and `PKCS #12` formats. 
- *Optional* - Include the private key in the export. Not supported with `DER` format.
- *Optional* - Provide a password to be used for the exported private key.
- *Optional* - Also store the export certificate response in `JSON` format in an [Octopus sensitive output variable](https://octopus.com/docs/projects/variables/output-variables#sensitive-output-variables). This output variable can then be used in additional deployment or runbook steps.
- *Optional* - on successful completion, you can revoke the access token used.

---

**Octopus Import options:**

- Octopus URL
- Octopus API Key
- Octopus Space name
- Certificate name
- *Optional* - replace the existing certificate if it already exists

---

**Required:** 
- The `VenafiPS` PowerShell module installed on the deployment target or worker. If the module can't be found, the step will attempt to download a version from the [PowerShell gallery](https://www.powershellgallery.com/packages/VenafiPS).
- PowerShell `5` or greater.
- Octopus API key with permission to save the certificate.

Notes:

- Tested on Octopus `2021.2`.
- Tested with VenafiPS `3.1.5`.
- Tested with both Windows PowerShell and PowerShell Core on Linux.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.Server
Parameters.Label: Venafi TPP Server
Parameters.HelpText:
*Required*: The URL of the Venafi TPP instance you want to export the certificate from.

For example: `https://mytppserver.example.com`.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.AccessToken
Parameters.Label: Venafi TPP Access Token
Parameters.HelpText: *Required*: The access token to authenticate against the TPP instance.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.DNPath
Parameters.Label: Venafi TPP Certificate Path
Parameters.HelpText: *Required*: The Distinguished Name (DN) of the certificate you wish to export. This is the absolute path to the certificate in the TPP instance, separated by `\`.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.Format
Parameters.Label: Certificate Export Format
Parameters.HelpText:
*Required*: The certificate export format. Valid options are:

- `Base64`
- `Base64 (PKCS #8)`
- `DER`
- `PKCS #12`

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.IncludeChain
Parameters.Label: Include certificate chain (Optional)
Parameters.HelpText: *Optional*: Include the certificate chain with the exported certificate. Not supported with `DER` format. Default: `False`.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.FriendlyName
Parameters.Label: Friendly Name (Optional)
Parameters.HelpText: *Optional*: Label or alias to use. Permitted with `Base64` and `PKCS #12` formats.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.IncludePrivateKey
Parameters.Label: Include Private Key (Optional)
Parameters.HelpText: *Optional*: Include the private key in the certificate export. If this is selected, the `Venafi.TPP.Export.PrivateKeyPassword` must also be provided. Default: `False`.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.PrivateKeyPassword
Parameters.Label: Private Key password (Optional)
Parameters.HelpText:
*Optional*: The password required to include the private key. Not supported with `DER` format.  You must adhere to the following rules: 

- Password is at least 12 characters. 
- Comprised of at least three of the following: 
  - Uppercase alphabetic letters 
  - Lowercase alphabetic letters 
  - Numeric characters 
  - Special characters

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.OutputVariableName
Parameters.Label: Certificate output variable name (Optional)
Parameters.HelpText: *Optional*: Create an output variable with the certificate details returned from the export call. The certificate details will be stored in `JSON` format.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.RevokeTokenOnCompletion
Parameters.Label: Revoke access token on completion?
Parameters.HelpText: *Optional*: Should the access token used be revoked once the step has been completed successfully? Default: `False`.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.OctopusServerUri
Parameters.Label: Octopus Server Url
Parameters.HelpText:
*Required*: Provide the base URL of your Octopus Server. There are two built-in Octopus variables you can use:

- `#{if Octopus.Web.ServerUri}#{Octopus.Web.ServerUri}#{else}#{Octopus.Web.BaseUrl}#{/if}`
- `Octopus.Web.ServerUri`

See our [system variables](https://octopus.com/docs/projects/variables/system-variables#Systemvariables-Server) page for further details.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.OctopusApiKey
Parameters.Label: Octopus API Key
Parameters.HelpText: *Required*: Provide an Octopus API Key with appropriate permissions to save the certificate.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.OctopusSpaceName
Parameters.Label: Octopus Space Name
Parameters.HelpText: *Required*: Provide the Space name for the certificate to be saved in. The default is the current space the step is running within: `#{Octopus.Space.Name}`.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.OctopusCertificateName
Parameters.Label: Octopus Certificate Name
Parameters.HelpText: *Required*: A short, memorable, unique name for the imported certificate.

Parameters.Name: Venafi.TPP.ImportCert.OctopusReplaceExistingCertificate
Parameters.Label: Replace existing Octopus certificate?
Parameters.HelpText:
*Optional*: If a certificate exists in Octopus with the same name as the one to be imported, should the one stored in Octopus be replaced? Default: `True`.

**Note**: If multiple matches are found, the step template will not replace any, and will log a warning instead.

See [replacing certificates](https://octopus.com/docs/deployments/certificates/replace-certificate) for further information.
